title: clinopodium serpyllifolium subsp. fruticosum
intro: Clinopodium serpyllifolium subsp. fruticosum, also known as Rock savory or Woodland savory, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mint family. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and carminative qualities, making it effective in treating digestive issues and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Clinopodium serpyllifolium subsp. fruticosum include its ability to alleviate symptoms of colds and flu, reduce fever, and ease nausea. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
